title: Helen Cho
content:
{{CharHub|Helen Cho|Helen Cho}} {{Character |image = Helen Cho.jpg |real name = Helen Cho (조헬렌) |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|ROK}} |gender = Female |title = Doctor |affiliation = [[U-GIN]] |movie = ''[[Avengers: Age of Ultron]]''
''[[Avengers: Endgame]]'' (picture; [[:File:Mind Stone Endgame.png|background screen]])[https://cantinacreative.com/endgame Avengers: Endgame - Cantina Creative] |book = ''[[The Wakanda Files: A Technological Exploration of the Avengers and Beyond]]'' |actor = [[Claudia Kim]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|The [[vibranium]] atoms aren't just compatible with the tissue cells, they're binding them. And [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] never even thought—"
"The most versatile substance on the [[Earth|planet]] and they used it to make a [[Captain America's Shield|frisbee]]. Typical of [[humans]].|Helen Cho and [[Ultron]]|Avengers: Age of Ultron}} Doctor '''Helen Cho''' (Korean: '''조헬렌''') is a world-renowned geneticist and the leader of the [[U-GIN]] Research Group. She was called to assist the [[Avengers]] with her research and technology in the [[War on Hydra|war]] against [[Hydra]], asking her to treat [[Clint Barton]]. Later, she was approached by [[Ultron]] who enthralled her to create [[Vision|a new body]] for him. However, [[Wanda Maximoff]] undid Cho's control and she managed to corrupt the process of the creation, although Ultron almost killed her for that. ==Biography== ===Working with the Avengers=== ====Saving Clint Barton==== {{Quote|The nano-molecular functionality is instantaneous. [[Hawkeye|His]] cells don't know they're bonding with a simulacrum.|Helen Cho to the [[Avengers]]|Avengers: Age of Ultron}} [[File:Hillcho.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Cho and [[Maria Hill]] await the [[Avengers]]]] Cho was called to [[Avengers Tower]] by [[Tony Stark]] after [[Clint Barton]] was injured in a [[Attack on the Hydra Research Base|battle]] against [[Hydra]]. She was invited to set up her equipment in [[Bruce Banner]]'s lab, where she had worked before and awaited the Avengers' arrival alongside [[Maria Hill]]. Once the Avengers arrived, Cho quickly addressed Barton's injuries and had him moved into the lab. [[File:Helen-Cho-saves-Hawkeye-AAoU.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Cho successfully saves [[Clint Barton]]'s life]] Cho used a machine based on the advanced technology she had developed in [[Seoul]] called the [[Regeneration Cradle]] to rebuild Barton's damaged skin, saving his life in mere minutes. Once Barton was stable and joking about living forever, Cho explained how the machine worked, greatly impressing Banner and the others watching. When Cho suggested that Barton's own girlfriend would not be able to tell the difference, Barton insisted he did not have a girlfriend. [[Tony Stark]] later invited Cho to a party the team was having to celebrate their victory. At first, she declined but changed her mind when she learned that [[Thor]] would be attending.{{Ref|AAoU}} ====Victory Celebration==== [[File:Helen-Cho-hides-AAoU.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Cho hides from [[Ultron]] during the fight]] {{Quote|I don't understand. You built this [[Ultron Program|program]]. Why is [[Ultron|it]] trying to kill us?|Helen Cho to [[Tony Stark]]|Avengers: Age of Ultron}} Cho attended the party in the [[Avengers Tower]] and stayed behind after many of the guests had returned home. During the after-party, many of the [[Avengers]] challenged themselves to lift [[Mjølnir]] while Cho watched. They were interrupted by [[Ultron]] who attacked the group. Cho found a place to hide behind a grand piano during the [[Attack on Avengers Tower|confrontation]]. When a corrupted [[Iron Legion]] drone attempted to kill Cho, Ultron signaled for it not to fire and spared her life. [[File:Helen-Cho-studies-Ultron.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Cho and the [[Avengers]] discuss [[Ultron]]]] Before long, the Avengers managed to overpower and defeat Ultron, although Ultron's mind disappeared through the internet. The group gathered in [[Tony Stark]]'s lab where they discussed what they had witnessed. Cho questioned why the [[Ultron Program|design]] Stark had built had turned against him and watched the Avengers turned against one another as they discussed how Stark and [[Bruce Banner]] could have created the being in the first place and what they could do to destroy it. While the Avengers traveled the world to hunt down and destroy Ultron, Cho returned to her lab in [[Seoul]] to continue her own work.{{Ref|AAoU}} ====Creating Vision==== [[File:SentryHelenCho-AoU.png|{{AltFloat}}|[[Ultron]] takes control over Cho's mind]] {{Quote|The [[Regeneration Cradle]] prints tissue. It can't build a living body."
"It can. You can. You lacked the [[Vibranium|materials]].|Helen Cho and [[Ultron]]|Avengers: Age of Ultron}} While working with her team of scientists on the [[U-GIN Genetic Research Facility]] in [[Seoul]], Cho returned to her lab to find [[Ultron]] was waiting for her with [[Wanda Maximoff|Wanda]] and [[Pietro Maximoff]]. Ultron explained to her the reason why he [[Attack on Avengers Tower|spared her life]], foreseeing Cho's usefulness in his future plans and threatened to kill her work staff if she alerted them. Ultron then used the [[Scepter]] on her to control her. [[File:Helen-Cho-helps-Ultron.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Cho helps [[Ultron]] build a new [[Vision|body]]]] Cho assisted in building a human-like body for Ultron using [[Vibranium]] and her technology from the [[Regeneration Cradle]], which was given life when the [[Mind Stone|gem]] from the Scepter was placed onto its forehead. When Wanda examined the Cradle as it was transferring Ultron's consciousness into the body, she saw that his main goal was to destroy all human life on [[Earth]]. Horrified, Wanda freed Cho from the hypnosis so that she could stop him. Cho recovered her senses and proceeded to disconnect Ultron from the uplink. She was consequently shot with a concussive blast while the Maximoff twins escaped. [[File:Helen-Cho-Rescued-by-Cap-AAoU.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Cho is rescued by [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]]] While Ultron attempted to escape with the Cradle, Cho recovered from her injuries. [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] later entered the facility and tended to her wounds; however, she told him about Ultron's plan to use the Cradle, warning Rogers that it must be recovered but not destroyed as the power inside the gemstone had the potential to destroy all of Seoul. Cho ultimately survived the blast and her work gave birth to the [[Vision]], a powerful android with the mind of [[J.A.R.V.I.S.]] who helped the [[Avengers]] destroy Ultron.{{Ref|AAoU}} ====Back in the Field==== [[File:Hillcho2.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Cho and Hill in the [[Avengers Compound]]]] Due to her impressive work, Cho was invited to work with the [[Avengers]] at their [[Avengers Compound]] after she fully recovered from her wounds. Cho and [[Maria Hill]] toured through the new laboratories filled with her team of scientists led by [[Erik Selvig]] as they began their work to assist the Avengers.{{Ref|AAoU}} ==Abilities== {{Quote|You're a brilliant woman, Helen.|[[Ultron]] to Helen Cho|Avengers: Age of Ultron}} *'''Genius-Level Intellect''': Cho is one of the smartest humans in the world, rivaled only by fellow geniuses like [[Tony Stark]], [[Bruce Banner]], and [[Shuri]]. {{Quote|She is creating tissue.|[[Bruce Banner]]|Avengers: Age of Ultron}} *'''Master Scientist''': [[File:Age-ultron-movie-screencaps.com-1972.jpg|thumb|250px|Cho heals [[Clint Barton]] with the [[Regeneration Cradle|Cradle]]]] Being a world-famous geneticist, Cho has tremendous knowledge and unmatched understanding in the fields of cell biology having invented the [[Regeneration Cradle]], a machine that can regenerate skin tissue, something that amazed even the likes of [[Bruce Banner]]. [[Ultron]] kept Cho alive when he realized how important she could have been to his plans. She also co-created [[Vision]], Ultron's desired body who ultimately became an [[Avengers|Avenger]]. *'''Master Physician''': Cho can apply her knowledge of cell biology and genetics for healing purposes, having created the [[Regeneration Cradle]] and other gadgetry highly useful to the Avengers for medical purposes. The cradle was eventually used to heal [[Clint Barton]]'s injury. *'''Bilingualism''': Cho is fluent in her native Korean, as well as English. ==Facilities== *'''[[U-GIN Genetic Research Facility]]''': Cho worked for [[U-GIN]] at their research facility, where she was brainwashed by [[Ultron]] to help him build a [[Vision|new body]]. The body was stolen by the [[Avengers]], who broke her mind control. *'''[[Avengers Tower]]''': Cho worked with the [[Avengers]] on her [[Regeneration Cradle]] at Avengers, which she used to heal [[Clint Barton]]'s injury. She attended a party at the tower, where she and the Avengers were attacked by [[Ultron]]. *'''[[Avengers Compound]]''': Cho worked with the Avengers at their compound, where she and [[Maria Hill]] toured through the laboratories. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Allies=== *[[U-GIN]] *[[Maya Hansen]] † - Former Colleague *[[John F. Burke]] - Colleague *[[Avengers]] **[[Steve Rogers|Steve Rogers/Captain America]] **[[Iron Man|Tony Stark/Iron Man]] † **[[Thor]] - Love Interest **[[Hulk|Bruce Banner/Hulk]] **[[Black Widow|Natasha Romanoff/Black Widow]] † **[[Hawkeye|Clint Barton/Hawkeye]] - Patient **[[Wanda Maximoff]] † - Former Enemy **[[Quicksilver|Pietro Maximoff/Quicksilver]] † - Former Enemy **[[Vision]] † - Co-Creation **[[Sam Wilson|Sam Wilson/Falcon]] **[[War Machine|James Rhodes/War Machine]] *[[Maria Hill]] † *[[Nick Fury]] *[[Erik Selvig]] - Colleague {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Ultron]] † - Former Enthraller and Attempted Killer **[[Ultron Sentries]] {{Col-end}} ==Trivia== *In the comics, Helen Cho is the mother of [[Amadeus Cho]]. ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} ==References== {{Gallery|Helen Cho/Gallery}} {{Wq|Helen Cho}} {{Reflist}} [[es:Helen Cho]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Avengers: Age of Ultron Characters]] [[Category:Book Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Brown Hair]] [[Category:Brown Eyes]] [[Category:Scientists]] [[Category:Physicians]] [[Category:U-GIN Leaders]] [[Category:Brainwashed Characters]] [[Category:Multilingual Characters]]
